mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications. It provides a lightweight and scalable solution for communication between services and devices. Key trade-offs include simplicity and performance versus advanced features found in more complex messaging systems.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/nats-helm

```

> This is the official repository for NATS Helm charts.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the NATS server using Helm, specifying the desired version and namespace.

```bash

helm install nats nats/nats --version 2.11.0 --namespace nats --create-namespace

```

> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create a NATS configuration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition (CRD) for NATS to configure its settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Look for the status of the NATS pods to be 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This command removes the NATS deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all NATS data.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> This will remove the NATS release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your NATS configuration. This step will upgrade the NATS server to a specified version.

```bash

kubectl get nats my-nats -o yaml > my-nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --version 2.11.0 --namespace nats

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Replace '2.11.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats my-nats -o yaml > my-nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to incorrect configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' for errors and adjust the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS server
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the NATS service is correctly configured and accessible from your client.

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The server may be overloaded or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource usage and logs to identify issues, and consider scaling the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: NATS client cannot publish messages
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the subject is incorrectly specified or permissions are misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the subject name and check the NATS permissions for the client.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for JetStream API, improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, review the JetStream configuration documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS server running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n nats' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
